title: Modern AI: Transformers and Large Language Models
description: How words become vectors, how attention and the transformer architecture work, and how large language models are trained, used, and can go wrong.
objectives: Explain how word embeddings represent meaning as vectors of numbers, so that similar words end up close together
Describe, in intuitive terms, how the attention mechanism lets each word weigh the relevance of every other word in a sequence
Describe the transformer architecture at a high level, including how it differs from older word-by-word (RNN) approaches
Explain what pretraining and fine-tuning are, and how together they produce a large language model
Identify well-known capabilities and limitations of LLMs, including hallucination, lack of true understanding, prompt sensitivity, and training-data bias

lessons.title: Word embeddings: representing meaning as vectors
Large language models: training, capabilities and limitations
